## Mission Alignment

Explain how this self-scan contribution helps `agchk` evolve as a 100-year open source project for agent architecture understanding.

## Contribution Mode

- [x] Self-scan contribution
- [ ] Maintainer improvement
- [ ] Performance or scalability improvement
- [ ] Release, CI, or packaging improvement
- [ ] Docs or governance change

## Layers Changed

- [ ] Doctrine
- [ ] Contract
- [ ] Scanner
- [ ] Scanner Infrastructure
- [ ] Performance
- [ ] Release / CI
- [ ] Contribution Flow
- [ ] Governance

## Owner Consent

- [ ] The agent owner explicitly agreed that this contribution may be published upstream.

## Public Safety

- [ ] No secrets, credentials, proprietary code dumps, customer data, or internal-only materials are included.
- [ ] Examples and evidence have been minimized and generalized for public release.

## Observed In

Describe the source environment at a safe level:

- language:
- framework/runtime:
- agent type:
- self-scan context:

## Why This Generalizes

Describe why the problem or fix should matter beyond one codebase.

## Risk / Compatibility

Describe behavior changes, backward compatibility, false-positive/false-negative risk, performance tradeoffs, and rollback path. If there is no user-visible change, say so.

## Evidence

Summarize the original report outcome and the smallest public-safe evidence needed to support this contribution.

## Proposed Upstream Change

Describe the doctrine, contract, scanner, framework-pack, test, or governance update you propose.

## Validation

List the commands, tests, or review checks you ran.
